Evolv Platform Job Aid

Teams, Alerts, Workflows & Tasks

A working reference for setting up Compliance.ai Evolv: how to build Teams, configure Alerts, design Workflows, and define Tasks — plus the templates and lookup lists you'll need along the way.

6Procedures
5Templates
11Reference lists
21,571Lookup values
Part One

Instructions

Step-by-step procedures for the core Evolv configuration tasks, in the order you'll typically use them.

Create Teams

19 steps

●Why this matters Draft
PurposeA team bundles a group of people with a shared slice of the regulatory landscape (its Default Filter) and a shared pool that workflows can assign tasks to. When to useStand up a new team when a function's regulatory exposure is genuinely different from the rest of the org — not just to mirror the org chart. BenefitKeeps monitoring targeted (people see only what matters to them) and keeps task assignment simple (assign to “the team,” not a list of names).

Teams in Evolv aren't just an org-chart mirror. Each team carries its own Default Filter — topics, agencies, document types, and so on — which becomes the lens that team's members see when they look at regulatory content, and the pre-set pool of people workflows can assign work to.

Creating a new team makes sense when a group's regulatory exposure is genuinely different from the rest of the organization — Finance doesn't need HR's labor-law alerts, and vice versa — or when a workflow needs a distinct pool of assignees. It's less useful purely as a way to organize people who all care about the same content; in that case a shared team with more members is simpler than several near-duplicate teams.

The main cost of getting the scope wrong runs in both directions: too broad and people drown in alerts that don't apply to them; too narrow and something that should have surfaced never does.

Draft — based on the workbook plus general compliance-workflow reasoning. Please review and edit before treating this as authoritative.

1

Navigate to the Compliance Assurance Hub and click on + Add team.

2

Enter a name for the Team and select an existing group or enter the name of the new group you would like to create.

3

Once the Team has been created, you may add members, modify the filter, edit the team name and group, and copy the team.

4

To add members to the Team, click on Members in the Team widget.

5

Next, you will add Members to the Team by clicking on Add member.

6

You will be able to enter the Evolv Member's email address in the Search field or select the member from the dropdown list.

7

After you have selected the Evolv Member, you will see the member's Name, Email, and Team associations appear.

8

To modify the Team Filter, click the Default Filter menu item.

9

Within the Default Filters, you are able to Add Areas of Focus and select the Topics, Regulatory Sources, News & Premium Content, and Document Types for the Team.

10

Areas of Focus - to add an Area of Focus, click + Add Area of Focus and select Add an existing area of focus or Create a new area of focus.

If you do not want or need to create a new Area of Focus, proceed to Step 13.

11

Add an existing area of focus - If you select this option, you will have the opportunity to select concepts from the Evolv repository.

  1. To find an area, scroll through the items in the left panel until you see the concepts on the right panel you wish to add.
  2. Click on the concept you want to add in the right panel (this action will place a checkmark next to the term - you may only choose one term).
  3. Click the Add button at the bottom of the dialog box.

NOTE: If you found the appropriate concept, proceed to Step 13. If you do not see a concept you would like to choose, click the Create my own link at the bottom of the dialog box and proceed to Step 12.

12

Create a new area of focus - If you select this option, you will complete the following information for the new focus area:

  1. Enter the Area of focus Name.
  2. Click the Create button.
  3. Click on the new Area of Focus in the sub-menu under Default Filters to configure the Topics, Regulatory Sources, News & Premium Content, and Document Types for this new area.
13

Topics - select the Topics and Concepts relevant to the Area of Focus or Initial Defaults by clicking on the topics and concept buttons. In the Concepts section, you may select all or just some of the concepts by clicking on the + on the right side of the button.

14

Regulatory Sources - to select regulatory sources for the Team's filter, click on the Regulatory Sources menu item and click + Add Country.

15

Add Country - scroll through the lists of countries, click the country you wish to add and click Add.

16

You will now see all of the agencies and territories associated with the country you selected. Select the applicable Agencies and Territories (States, Provinces, etc.). You may also search for Other Sources in addition to the listed Agencies.

17

Other Sources - to select other sources (Agencies) for the country you selected, click in the Other Sources search field. A list of all agencies and institutions known to Evolv will be displayed. You may also search this listing by typing part or all of the agencies/institution name in the search field.

18

News & Premium Content - to select news sources, click the News & Premium Content menu item and click the button for each of the applicable news sources you wish to add to your Team's filter.

19

Document Types - to select the document types you wish to include in the Team's filter, click on the Document Types menu item and select the applicable document types. You may select all or just some of the document types by clicking on the + on the right side of the button.

Searching — Create an Alert

8 steps

●Why this matters Draft
PurposeAn alert is a saved search — it defines, once, the exact slice of regulatory content a team or workflow should watch, using any combination of the available filters. When to useUse search-built alerts for ongoing, repeatable monitoring, or whenever a workflow needs a defined document population to run against — not for a one-off lookup. BenefitThe same criteria run every time; a workflow built on an alert only ever sees documents that match it, so search precision directly controls how much work downstream teams get handed.

An alert is a saved search: everything you set in the filter panel — agency, jurisdiction, topic, document type, date ranges, and so on — gets stored as a standing query rather than a one-time lookup. That's what a workflow attaches to when you select an associated alert in Workflow Settings: the workflow only ever acts on documents the alert would have surfaced.

How the system combines filters: most systems built this way apply AND across different filter categories (a document must match the agency filter and the topic filter) and OR within a single category (any of the agencies you selected). This is the standard pattern for faceted search, but it isn't confirmed against Evolv's own documentation — verify it against a few test alerts before relying on it for a business-critical scope.

Because the alert defines the workflow's entire document population, time spent narrowing the search well is rarely wasted — a scope that's too broad turns every matching document into unnecessary tasks for a team.

Draft — based on the workbook plus general compliance-workflow reasoning. Please review and edit before treating this as authoritative.

1

Navigate to Regulatory Intelligence.

2

Navigate to Horizon Scanning Timeline (this step is optional - search can be done from any area in the Evolv system).

3

Click the Filter to open the filter criteria options.

4

Ajust the filter criteria options and click Search to see the results.

Go to the Search Filters tab in this workbook for detailed information about search filter options.

5

Review the resulting documents and update the Filter criteria as needed to produce the appropriate document set.

6

Once your filter criteria has produced the desired document set, click Create Alert.

7

Enter a name for the Alert and click Create.

8

In the You've created a new alert dialog box, click GO TO MANAGE WORKFLOW to add the alert to a Workflow.

Create a Workflow

6 steps

●Why this matters Draft
PurposeA workflow is the automation layer that takes an alert's search results and turns them into a structured, trackable process: what happens, to which documents, and who's in scope. When to useCreate a workflow when a category of regulatory document needs a repeatable internal review or response process — not for one-off, ad hoc document reviews. BenefitThe same process runs every time a matching document appears, ownership is explicit (which team, which people), and there's a record of what was done and when.

A workflow is where an alert's results become work. Its settings decide whether tasks are generated per document or per obligation within a document (the field guide notes the obligation-level option can multiply into a very large number of tasks, so entire-document is the more common default), which teams are in scope, and which alert defines the population it acts on.

Workflows are worth building when a document type recurs and needs the same internal handling every time — a new rule from a given agency always needs review, sign-off, and filing, for example. They're the wrong tool for a single ad hoc review that won't repeat; a workflow's value is in the consistency it enforces across many future documents, not in any one instance of it.

Because a workflow is only as good as the alert feeding it and the tasks defined inside it, most of the actual design work happens in those two places — the workflow itself is mostly the wiring between them.

Draft — based on the workbook plus general compliance-workflow reasoning. Please review and edit before treating this as authoritative.

1

If you clicked on GO TO MANAGE WORKFLOW after creating an Alert, you may skip this step.

Navigate to Tasks and Processes and click on Manage Workflow.

2

Click on Add Workflow.

3

If this is your first Workflow, you will select Start from scratch.

4

Enter a name for the new Workflow.

5

Next, complete the Workflow Settings.

  1. Do you want to complete your workflow for the entire document, for obligations, or both? - the default for this setting is Entire document. The Entire document option is recommended in most situations. If you choose to complete the workflow for each Obligation, keep in mind that a very large number of tasks could be created.
  2. Select associated alert / notifications for this workflow - select a previously created alert that has been configured to search for the documents upon which the workflow will be performed.
  3. Select teams - select one or more previously configured teams that will be in scope for the workflow.
  4. Select collection - select the previously configured Collection you wish to assign the workflow to.
  5. Published since - this field is by default set to the date the workflow was created. You may select an alternate date if needed.
  6. Allow workflow to be paused - click the check box for this option if you wish to pause the operation of the workflow.
  7. Task Settings - clicking the check box for Don't let assignees work on downstream (i.e., blocked/dependent) tasks until upstream tasks are completed will prevent assignees from completing any tasks that are downstream of a task that has not yet been completed.
  8. Choose who can update due dates for tasks - this field enables you to select the roles that may update task due dates.
6

Once the Workflow information has been provided, click on + Add a Task to begin creating tasks for the workflow. Click on the Create a Task Tab for instructions on creating tasks.

Create a Task

18 steps

●Why this matters Draft
PurposeIndividual tasks are the actual units of accountable work inside a workflow — due dates, evidence fields, approval gates, and dependencies on other tasks all live here. When to useBreak a workflow into multiple tasks when different people need to do different things in sequence, when you need a sign-off step, or when later work should depend on an earlier answer. BenefitMakes accountability and deadlines explicit per person rather than per workflow, and gives you a built-in control point (approval) before something counts as done.

Tasks are where a workflow's intent turns into individually assigned, dated, trackable work. A task's due date can be tied to a document's own dates (publication, effective) or, for downstream tasks, to the completion of an earlier task — which is what lets a workflow model a real sequence (review, then approve, then file) instead of assigning everything at once.

The approval fields (require approval, approver, primary approver) exist for the tasks where "someone marked it done" isn't sufficient assurance — add them where a second set of eyes is genuinely part of your control environment, not by default on every task, since each approval step adds a person to the critical path.

Task dependency (making a task relevant or not based on a prior task's response) is worth using when a workflow has real branches — e.g., a task should only exist if an earlier task determined the document applies to a particular business line. For workflows without real branching, plain sequential tasks are easier to reason about than dependency logic.

Draft — based on the workbook plus general compliance-workflow reasoning. Please review and edit before treating this as authoritative.

1

After clicking Add Task in the Workflow screen, you will see the Task configuration screen.

2

Enter a title for the task in Title. The title should be brief but provide enough information to determine the purpose of the task.

3

Enter the following information to determine the Task Due Date.

  1. Due by - enter a numerical value to indicate the number of days before or after a particular date the task must be completed.
  2. Before/After - select Before or After in the field next to days.
  3. Select one of the document or workflow date categories:
  1. Publication Date
  2. Effective Date
  3. Comments Close
  4. Task Creation

NOTE: In subsequent tasks, an additional date category, Task Completion, will be available for selection. If you are creating a subsequent tasks, you have the option to select an upstream task under the Task Completion heading.

4

Select the Task Assignees:

  1. Teams - If you previously defined one or more Teams, you may select a team from the Teams drop-down. This will not assign the Task to a Team, it will only filter the values that will display in the Assign to and Notify drop-downs.
  2. Assign to - select the Evolv Member(s) who will be required to complete the task.
  3. Notify - select the Evolv Member(s) who should be notified about the Task.
5

Enter a Description for the Task. The description should sufficently inform the recipient of why they are required to complete the Task.

6

Indicate whether approval(s) are required to extend the Task Due Date. If no approvals are required to extend the due date, leave the Require approval to extend the due date unchecked. If you wish to require approval(s) for due date extension, click the check box next to Require approval to extend the due date and provide the following information:

  1. Max extension <#> days - enter a numerical value indicating the maximum number of days that the due date for the task can be extended.
  2. Needs approval from (Approvers & Primary Approver) - select the Evolv Members who can approve the extension and the Evolv Member that is the primary approver (only one member may be selected as Primary).
  3. and needs to be approved within <#> days - enter a numerical value indicating the number of days within which approval must be granted.

4. + Add another extension - if you wish to allow additional extensions, click
+ Add another extension and provide additional extension detail (steps 1 - 3).

7

You may choose to require an approval before a task can be marked complete. Selecting Require approval allows you to add an approver to the task. The primary task assignee will be able to submit for approval, but will not be able to complete the task until the task is approved. Once the task is submitted for approval, it is assigned to the approver. The approver can then approve and complete the task or reject and return the task to the original assignee. To set an approval requirement on the task, complete the following:

  1. Click the check box for Require approval.
  2. Select Approver - select one or more Evolv Members as the Approver from the drop-down.
  3. Select Primary Approver - select one Evolv Member as the Primary Approver from the drop-down.

Task Fields: You will need to indicate the fields to be completed for the task. You have the option of adding a text field, an attachment field, multiple drop-down fields, and date field. Each of these field types will be explained in the following steps.

8

Add a Text field - if you wish to add a field for free form text to the task (the text field will be titled as Comments in the task), click the check box next to Text field. The assignee will be able to use this field to provide comments, additional information about their review, etc.

You may choose to make the Text field required by clicking the check box next to Required. By default, this option is unchecked.

9

Add an Attachments field - if you wish to add a field for the assignee to attach external files to the task, click the check box next to Attachments. The assignee will be able to use this field to attach external files (from their local computer, etc.).

You may choose to make the Attachments field required by clicking the check box next to Required. By default, this option is unchecked.

10

Add dropdown field - dropdown fields may be used to indicate the status of the task, different attributes regarding the document or obligation being reviewed, document classification, etc. To add a dropdown field, click + Add a dropdown field link and provide the following:

  1. Dropdown Label - enter a label (name) for the dropdown field. The label should be descriptive enough that the assignee will easily understand the intention of the field.
  2. Required - if the field must be completed before the task can be marked complete, click the check box next to Required.
  3. Multiselect - if you wish to allow the assignee to select more than one of the dropdown options, click the check box next to Multiselect.
  4. Dropdown Options - these are the values that will be selectable for the Dropdown field. The values should be descriptive enough that the assignee will be able to easily understand the meaning of each. To add additional Dropdown Options, click the + Add dropdown option link and add the additional value(s).
  5. To continue adding Dropdown fields, click the + Add dropdown field link.

NOTE: If you intend to use a dropdown field to define a cross-task dependency, that dropdown must be the first dropdown created or moved to the top (with the reorder feature).

Example
  1. Click + Add a dropdown field
  2. Enter the Dropdown Label, indicate whether the field will be Required, indicate whether the field values will be Multiselect, and add Dropdown Options.
11

Add date field - date fields may be used to indicate the date an event (such as a review, etc.) was completed. To add a date field, click + Add date field link and provide the following:

  1. Date Field Label - enter a label (name) for the date field. The label should be descriptive enough that the assignee will easily understand the intention of the field.
  2. Required - if the field must be completed before the task can be marked complete, click the check box next to Required.
Example
  1. Click + Add date field.
  2. Enter the Date Field Label and indicate whether the field will be Required.
12

Select Assignees - this option enables the task assignee (of the current task) to select one or more Evolv Members who should complete the next downstream task. To enable subsequent tasks to be dynamically assigned:

  1. Click the check box next to Select assignees.
  2. Select the assignee(s) from the Select Assignee drop down.

It is important to remember that the Assign to field in subsequent tasks should be set to Dependent on and the appropriate task selected in the Dependent on field. This will be covered in depth in another future step.

13

Adding additional Tasks - workflows will typically have multiple tasks. To add an additional task, click + Add a Task.

14

Downstream Tasks - Completion Date - generally, you will configure the new task the same way you configured the first task with a few exceptions. Follow Steps 2, 3, & 5 through 12. You will skip Step 4 as this step will be configured differently due to the Select Assignees option that was set in Step 12.

NOTE: As noted in Step 3, you have an additional Completion Date option in downstream tasks. The new option, Task Completion, allows you to base the task completion due date on the completion of an upstream task.

15

Downstream Tasks - Dependencies & Assignees - when you have completed the steps to configure the task (as defined in Steps 2, 3, & 5 through 12), you will need to configure the Task Assignees. On downstream tasks, you have the option to set the Assign to field to either an Evolv Member or Dependent on an upstream task.

In the first task, the Select assignees option was selected which indicates that the assignee on the most recent upstream task has the ability to select the assignees of the next downstream task. To configure this downstream task so that the upstream task assignee can select assignees for this task:

  1. Select Dependent on... from the Assign to dropdown.
  2. Select the name of the upstream task where the Select Assignees option was selected.
16

Downstream Task Dependency - to make the current task Relevant or Not Relevant based on a response provided in a prior task (a task completed earlier in the workflow), set the slidebar to "on" on the Downstream task dependency option. Once the Downstream task dependency option is enabled, you will need to configure the following:

  1. Make this task (meaning the task you are configuring the Downstream task dependency option on) - this field has the options Relevant or Not Relevant.
  2. If <All, Any> of the following match - this field has the options All or Any. The current task will be set as Relevant or Not Relevant based on the responses to All or Any of the responses in one or more prior tasks.
  3. <Tasks> is marked as <Answer> - in this section, you will select the prior task(s) and the answer to the first dropdown field for the prior task which will indicate whether the current task is Relevant or Not Relevant.

Example:

In the example shown in column C, we have configured the Downstream task dependency option on the Compliance Manager Review task to be Not Relevant if the Compliance Generalist Review (which occurs before the Compliance Manager Review) is marked as Document Not Relevant. This means that if the user completed the Compliance Generalist Review task and selected "Document Not Relevant" in their first drop down field, the Compliance Manager Review task will not need to be completed by the assigned user (it will be automatically completed by the Evolv system).

17

Upstream Task Dependency - to be able to make a task that is earlier in the workflow reopen based on a selection made of the current task, set the slidebar to "on" on the Upstream task dependency option. Once the Upstream task dependency is enabled, you will need to configure the following options:

  1. Change this task to incomplete <task> - select a previously completed task you wish to reopen if the current task (the task you are currently working in) is responded to in a particular way.
  2. If this task is marked as - select the answer in the first dropdown field of the current task that should cause a previously completed task to be marked as incomplete (reopened).

Example:

In the example shown in column C, we have configured the Upstream task dependency option on the Compliance Manager Review task so that if the user completing Compliance Manager Review task (the current task) selects "Return Document to Compliance Generalist" in the Compliance Manager Review Status field, the Compliance Generalist Review task will be reopened and the user will need to complete that task again.

Update Task Notifications

2 steps

●Why this matters Draft
PurposePersonal, per-user control over how often someone is emailed about tasks assigned to them or to their organization. When to useEvery user should set this once, early on; revisit it if someone is missing deadlines (too infrequent) or drowning in email (too frequent). BenefitLets people who want real-time visibility have it, and lets high-volume teams switch to a digest — without an admin needing to set an org-wide policy that Evolv doesn't support anyway.

Task notification cadence is set per person, not per organization — there's no admin control that overrides it. That makes it worth walking through during onboarding rather than leaving it on whatever default a new account starts with.

The practical trade-off is volume versus latency: individual task emails mean nothing is missed but a busy queue turns into constant interruptions; a daily or weekly summary trades some immediacy for a single, scannable digest. Teams with a high volume of recurring tasks tend to prefer a summary cadence; someone owning a small number of high-stakes tasks may prefer individual emails so nothing sits unnoticed.

Draft — based on the workbook plus general compliance-workflow reasoning. Please review and edit before treating this as authoritative.

1

Each user has the ability to set their own cadence for Task notifications. Currently, notifications cannot be globally set for the organization.

Users may set their notification preferences by navigating to Reporting, then under Regulatory Reports select the Tasks menu item.

2

Select the notification cadence that best fits your individual requirements.

  1. Task Email Cadence - for How would you like to receive task emails, you may select Individual task emails, Daily task summary emails, or Weekly task summary emails.
  2. Your Tasks & Organization Tasks (notify) - you are able to select one or more of the following options:
  1. Tasks Assigned
  2. Due in 24 hours
  3. Past Due
  4. Task Complete
  1. Tagged in a comment on tasks - you have the option to select Email me every time I'm tagged and/or Daily Summary.
  2. Tasks reassigned to you & Tasks reassigned from you - for these tasks, you have the option to select Individual email only.

Update System Notifications

2 steps

●Why this matters Draft
PurposePersonal control over broader system-generated notifications — regulatory, agency, and policy updates, plus product announcements — separate from task-specific notifications. When to useSame as task notifications: an individual, once-set preference, adjusted if the volume stops matching how closely someone needs to track the regulatory landscape. BenefitKeeps people informed at a cadence that matches their role — daily for someone actively monitoring a fast-moving area, weekly for someone who checks in periodically — without one global setting forcing everyone to the same rhythm.

System notifications cover the ongoing stream of regulatory activity — topic, agency, enforcement, and policy updates — plus separate, optional product announcements. Like task notifications, this is an individual setting with no organization-wide override.

The cadence choice mostly comes down to how central regulatory monitoring is to someone's day-to-day role: someone whose job is to watch a specific regulatory area closely benefits from a daily cadence so nothing sits stale; someone for whom this is peripheral context is usually better served by a weekly summary they can skim.

Draft — based on the workbook plus general compliance-workflow reasoning. Please review and edit before treating this as authoritative.

1

Each user has the ability to set their own cadence for System Generated notifications. Currently, notifications cannot be globally set for the organization.

Users may set their notification preferences by navigating to Reporting, then under Regulatory Reports select the System Generated menu item.

2

Select the notification cadence that best fits your individual requirements. For System Email Notifications and Glassdoor Email Notifications, you may select a Daily or Weekly cadence. For Product Notifications, you may opt in to receive them as they are published or not at all.

  1. System Email Notifications - include notifications regarding Topic, Agency, Enforcements and Policy updates.
  2. Glassdoor Email Notifications - include Glassdoor updates.
  3. Product Notifications - you may opt in/out as these notifications do not follow a particular cadence.
Part Two

Templates

Use these to document Teams, Alerts, Workflows, and Tasks outside of Evolv — each row below explains what a field is for and what to enter.

Team Definitions

FieldWhat to enter
Associated Workflow Name

Enter the name of any associated workflows (See the Workflow Definitions Tab)
Team Name

Enter the name of the team
(Instructions - see Create Teams Tab - Step 2)
Group

Enter an existing Group Name or New Group Name
(Instructions - see Create Teams Tab - Step 2)
SCIM Group(if applicable)

If applicable, enter the SCIM Group Name
Members

Enter the Members (email address) who should be part of the team
(Instructions - see Create Teams Tab - Step 3)
Initial Defaults - Topics(click on the Topics Tab Link above to see available topics)

Topics Tab Link
Initial Defaults - Regulatory Sources(click on the Agency Tab Link above to see available agencies)

Topics Tab Link
Initial Defaults - News & Premium Content(click on the News & Premium Tab Link above to see available news & premium content sources)

News & Premium Content Tab Link
Initial Defaults - Document Types(click on the Document Types Tab Link above to see available document types)

Document Types Tab Link
Additional Area of Focus Name

Instructions - see Create Teams Tab - Steps 9 through 12
<New Area of Focus> - Topics(click on the Topics Tab Link above to see available topics)

Topics Tab Link
<New Area of Focus> - Regulatory Sources(click on the Agency Tab Link above to see available agencies)

Topics Tab Link
<New Area of Focus> - News & Premium Content(click on the News & Premium Tab Link above to see available news & premium content sources)

News & Premium Content Tab Link
<New Area of Focus> - Document Types(click on the Document Types Tab Link above to see available document types)

Document Types Tab Link

Alert Definitions

FieldWhat to enter
Alert Name

Enter the name of the Alert
Publication Date

Select a date option from the cell dropdown
Publication Date Dates or Number of Days, Weeks, Months, or Years

If one of the following options was selected in the Publication Date column, enter the date(s) or number of days:

  1. Between: enter date 1 - date 2
  2. On: enter the date
  3. On or Before: enter the date
  4. On or After: enter the date
  5. In the last: enter number and days, weeks, months, or years (1 Days, 1 Weeks, etc.)
Added to Compliance.ai

Select a date option from the cell dropdown
Add to Compliance.ai Dates or Number of Days, Weeks, Months, or Years

If one of the following options was selected in the Added to Compliance.ai column, enter the date(s) or number of days:

  1. Between: enter date 1 - date 2
  2. On: enter the date
  3. On or Before: enter the date
  4. On or After: enter the date
  5. In the last: enter number and days, weeks, months, or years (1 Days, 1 Weeks, etc.)
Updated in Compliance.ai

Select a date option from the cell dropdown
Updated in Compliance.ai Dates or Number of Days, Weeks, Months, or Years

If one of the following options was selected in the Updated in Compliance.ai column, enter the date(s) or number of days:

  1. Between: enter date 1 - date 2
  2. On: enter the date
  3. On or Before: enter the date
  4. On or After: enter the date
  5. In the last: enter number and days, weeks, months, or years (1 Days, 1 Weeks, etc.)
Agency

Agency Tab Link
Exclude Federal Register

If this option is unchecked, Federal Register related documents will be included (if applicable). If this option is checked, Federal Register related documents will not be included.

Select option in cell dropdown

Yes - Exclude Federal Register
No - Include Federal Register
Jurisdiction

Jurisdictions Tab Link
News & Premium Content

News & Premium Content Tab Link
Topics

Topics Tab Link
Concepts

Concepts Tab Link
Author

Enter the Author's name
Document Properties

Select the document properties from the cell dropdown
Text is

If you wish to search based on text within a particular document, enter the text to search for
Title is

If you wish to search based on the document title, enter text to search for
Effective Date

Select a date option from the cell dropdown
Effective Date or Number of Days, Weeks, Months, or Years

If one of the following options was selected in the Effective Date column, enter the date(s) or number of days:

  1. Between: enter date 1 - date 2
  2. On: enter the date
  3. On or Before: enter the date
  4. On or After: enter the date
  5. In the last: enter number and days, weeks, months, or years (1 Days, 1 Weeks, etc.)
  6. In the next: enter number and days, weeks, months, or years (1 Days, 1 Weeks, etc.)
Comments Close Date

Select a date option from the cell dropdown
Comments Close Date or Number of Days, Weeks, Months, or Years

If one of the following options was selected in the Comments Close Date column, enter the date(s) or number of days:

  1. Between: enter date 1 - date 2
  2. On: enter the date
  3. On or Before: enter the date
  4. On or After: enter the date
  5. In the last: enter number and days, weeks, months, or years (1 Days, 1 Weeks, etc.)
  6. In the next: enter number and days, weeks, months, or years (1 Days, 1 Weeks, etc.)

If you wish to search based on a document ID, enter text to search for

If you wish to search based on a citation or internal ID, enter text to search for

If you wish to search based on a citation identifier, enter text to search for

Workflow Definitions

FieldWhat to enter
Workflow Name

Enter the name of the Workflow

(Instructions - see Create a Workflow Tab - Step 4)
Workflow Applicability(Entire Document or Obligation)

Select "Entire Document" or "Obligation" from the dropdown below to indicate whether the workflow should apply to individual obligations or to an entire document.

(Instructions - see Create a Workflow Tab - Step 5)
Associated Alert(Alert Name)

Enter the name of the associated Alert (see the Alert Definitions Tab)

(Instructions - see Create a Workflow Tab - Step 5)
Team(Team Name)

Enter the name of the associated Team (see the Team Definitions Tab)

(Instructions - see Create a Workflow Tab - Step 5)
Collection(Collection Name)

Enter the name of the Collection the workflow should be associated with.

(Instructions - see Create a Workflow Tab - Step 5)
Block Downstream Tasks Until Upstream Tasks Complete(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" or "No" in the dropdown below to indicate if downstream tasks should be blocked until an upstream task is completed.

(Instructions - see Create a Workflow Tab - Step 5)
Roles Allowed to Update Due Dates for Tasks(Org, Team, or Workflow Admins)

Select the admin role that is allowed to update due dates for tasks

(Instructions - see Create a Workflow Tab - Step 5)

Workflow Applicability

Workflow Applicability
Entire Document
Obligation

Roles Allowed to Update Due Dates

Roles Allowed to Update Due Dates for Tasks
Org Admins
Team Admins
Workflow Admins

Task Definitions

FieldWhat to enter
Workflow Name

Enter the name of the associated Workflow (see the Workflow Definitions Tab for Workflow Names)
Task Title

Enter a descriptive title for the task

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 2)
Due by # of Days

Enter a numerical value for the number of days before/after a specified event the task will be due.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 3)
Before/After

Select "Before" or "After" from the dropdown below to indicate if the due date will be before or after the condition selected in column E.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 3)
Date

Select one of the items in the dropdown below to indicate the event upon which the due date should be determined.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 3)
Task Name(only complete this column if you selected Task Completion: <Task Name> in column E)

If "Task Completion: <Task Name>" was selected in Column E, enter the name of the task that should be used to determine the due date.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 3)
Teams(Previously Configured Teams)

Enter the name of the associated Team (see the Team Definitions Tab). Selecting a Team does not assign the task to a team, it only filters the "Assign to" and "Notify" fields with the Members associated with the team.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 4)
Assign to(Evolv Member)

Enter the email address of the Member who should be assigned to the task.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 4)
Notify(Evolv Member)

Enter the email address of the Member who should be notified about the task.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 4)
Description(Task Description)

Enter a task description that contains adequate information for the task assignee to understand the task requirements.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 5)
Require Approval to Extend the Due Date (Yes or No)

Select "Yes" or "No" from the dropdown below to indicate whether approval is required to extend the task due date.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 6)
Max Extension <#> Days(Numerical Value)

If Yes was entered for column K, enter a numerical value that represents the maximum number of days the task completion date can be extended.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 6)
Needs Approval From <Approvers>(Evolv Members)

Enter the email address of the Member(s) who may approve the task due date extension. If more than one Member is entered, separate the email addresses with a ";".

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 6)
Primary Approver(Evolv Member)

Enter the email address of the Member who should be the primary approver for the extension of the task due date.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 6)
And Needs to be Approved within <#> Days(Numerical Value)

Enter a numerical value that represents the number of days within which the task due date extension request must be approved.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 6)
Require Approval(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" or "No" from the dropdown below to indicate whether the task must be approved after the assignee completes the task.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 7)
Select Approver(Evolv Member)

Enter the email address of the Member(s) who should approve the task after the assignee completes it. If more than one Member is entered, separate the email addresses with a ";".

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 7)
Primary Approver(Evolv Member)

Enter the email address of the Member who should be the primary approver of the task once the assignee completes it.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 7)
Text Field(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" if a text field should be created within the task or "No", if a text field is not needed in the dropdown below. A text field can be used for the assignee to enter notes/comments about the task.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 8)
Text Field Required(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" if the task assignee should be required to complete the text field (created in Column S) or "No", if a text field should not be required in the dropdown below.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 8)
Attachments Field(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" if an Attachments field should be created within the task or "No", if an Attachments field is not needed in the dropdown below. An Attachment field can be used for the assignee to attach documents related to the task.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 9)
Attachments Field Required(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" if the task assignee should be required to attach a document in the Attachments field (created in Column U) or "No", if the Attachments field should not be required in the dropdown below.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 9)
Dropdown Field(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" if a Dropdown field should be created within the task or "No", if a Dropdown field is not needed in the dropdown below. A dropdown field can be used for the assignee to indicate that the task has been completed or to alter the flow of a previous or upcoming task.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 10)
Dropdown Field Name(Enter a Field Name - multiple dropdown fields may be created for a single task)

Enter the Dropdown field name if "Yes" was selected in column W.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 10)
Dropdown Field Required?(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" if the task assignee should be required to select a value in the dropdown field (created in Column W) or "No", if the Dropdown field should not be required in the dropdown below.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 10)
Dropdown Field Options(Dropdown Field Values)

Enter the values that should be available for the Task Assignee to select from the dropdown field (if "Yes" was selected in column W). Enter the values for the dropdown field separated by ";" (e.g., Document Relevant; Document Not Relevant)

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 10)
Date Field(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" if a Date field should be created within the task or "No", if a Date field is not needed in the dropdown below. A date field can be used for the assignee to indicate the date the task was completed or other pertinent date information.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 11)
Date Field Name

Enter a name for the Date field if "Yes" was selected in column Z.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 11)
Date Field Required(Yes or No)

Select "Yes" if the task assignee should be required to enter a date in the Date field (created in Column Z) or "No", if the Date field should not be required in the dropdown below.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 11)
Select Assignees(Enter the email addresses of the Members who will need to complete the next downstream task separated by ";")

This option enables the task assignee to select one or more Evolv Members who should complete the next downstream task. A separate task will be created for each Member selected in the "Select Assignees" field. Enter the Member email addresses separated by ";".

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 12)
Downstream Task Dependency (Yes or No)

The Downstream Task Dependency option can be used to make the current task Relevant or Not Relevant based on a response provided in a prior task (a task completed earlier in the workflow).

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 16)
Make this Task

The Downstream Task Dependency option can be used to make the current task Relevant or Not Relevant based on a response provided in a prior task (a task completed earlier in the workflow).

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 16)
If <Any or All> of the Following Match(Any or All)

The Downstream Task Dependency option can be used to make the current task Relevant or Not Relevant based on a response provided in a prior task (a task completed earlier in the workflow).

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 16)
Downstream Task(s) Name(List Names of Downstream Tasks)

The Downstream Task Dependency option can be used to make the current task Relevant or Not Relevant based on a response provided in a prior task (a task completed earlier in the workflow).

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 16)
is Marked as <Answer>(Answer to first Dropdown field in associated Downstream Tasks)

The Downstream Task Dependency option can be used to make the current task Relevant or Not Relevant based on a response provided in a prior task (a task completed earlier in the workflow).

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 16)
Upstream Task Dependency(Yes or No)

The Upstream Task Dependency option can be used to to make a task that is earlier in the workflow reopen based on a selection made of the current task.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 17)
Change this task to Incomplete <Downstream Task Name>(List Name of Downstream Task)

The Upstream Task Dependency option can be used to to make a task that is earlier in the workflow reopen based on a selection made of the current task.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 17)
If this task is marked as <Answer>(Answer to first Dropdown field in associated Downstream Task)

The Upstream Task Dependency option can be used to to make a task that is earlier in the workflow reopen based on a selection made of the current task.

(Instructions - see Create a Task Tab - Step 17)

Due Date Before or After

Due Date Before or After
Before
After

Completion Dates
Document Dates:Publication Date
Document Dates:Effective Date
Document Dates:Comments Close
Task Completion:<Task Name>
Workflow Dates:Task Creation

Searchable lookup lists for every filter and pick-list value in Evolv. Type in any box to search — results update as you type.
